title: grossularia × rustica
intro: Grossularia × rustica, also known as wood sorrel or wild sorrel, is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Oxalidaceae. It has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues, as it contains oxalic acid and other compounds that have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Grossularia × rustica include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce inflammation, and support immune function. The plant can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, salves, and tinctures, and is often consumed as a culinary herb in soups and salads.
